windows.embed_text_v2: intro b-roll finished container candles on bedding, small dog sniffing scented jars, then overhead empty clear glass jar on granite with metal wick tabs being placed, prepare_container start
windows.summary_v2: Intro b-roll: finished store-bought container candles on a bed and a Yorkshire terrier dog inspecting them, ending on an overhead of metal wick-tabs placed inside an empty glass jar on a counter.

tags_v2.action: showing finished candles and starting wick placement
tags_v2.on_screen_text: Lilac; HOME sweet cinnamon vanilla (product labels)

seconds_v2.desc: Close-up of finished store-bought container candles stacked on a white bedsheet, the top one labeled 'Lilac' with blue floral artwork.
Camera tilts/pans across the same Lilac jar candle and another labeled product on white bedding.
Wider shot of three finished candle jars on the bed; a small Yorkshire terrier dog sniffs at one labeled 'HOME sweet cinnamon vanilla'.
Same scene, the dog continues to investigate the HOME-labeled candle jar.
Top-down look inside a clear glass jar containing a teal/blue half-burnt pillar candle with two wick stubs.
Blurry motion shot of the same teal candle jar on the white bedding as camera moves.
Yorkshire terrier looks directly at the camera, its face filling much of the frame next to the candle jars.
Dog turns its head, mouth open, jars still in left of frame.
Cut to overhead of an empty clear glass jar on a granite counter, with several metal wick-tabs sitting in the bottom being arranged for a new candle pour.

windows.transcript: If you were like me, the new hate wasting the leftover wax in the bath and bodyworks candles, I found a solution and I wanted to go ahead and
windows.embed_text_v2: showcasing leftover wax in several used Bath & Body Works glass jar candles, panning overhead across multi-wick jars of pale blue and cream wax with decorative lids on a white bedsheet
windows.summary_v2: Overhead B-roll pans across several part-used Bath & Body Works glass jar candles with leftover wax laid out on a white bedsheet before being repurposed.

tags_v2.action: displaying used jar candles with leftover wax
tags_v2.on_screen_text: product labels: Lilac Blossom, Beach Cabana, HOME sweet caramel, Honey

seconds_v2.desc: Overhead close-up of a clear glass jar candle holding pale blue leftover wax with three small burned wicks; a patterned product label and a decorative lid sit on the white bedsheet behind it.
Same pale-blue multi-wick glass candle, slight camera drift; the printed jar label and a textured silver lid remain visible against the white cloth.
Camera tilts down the side of the glass jar, revealing a red-white-and-blue striped label near its base and the burned wax surface inside on the wrinkled white sheet.
View shifts to a stack of finished store candles: a smaller blue-and-white 'Lilac Blossom' jar perched atop two larger jars on the bedsheet.
Three Bath & Body Works jar candles grouped together, faces toward camera: a striped blue 'Beach Cabana' jar, a green-and-orange 'HOME sweet caramel' jar, and the lilac jar behind them.
Camera pans low across the bottoms of the 'Beach Cabana' and 'HOME' jars, showing their paper base stickers and leftover wax through the glass on the white sheet.
Pan continues back over the clustered candles as a blurred bare hand briefly enters at upper right; the striped, lilac, and 'HOME' jars are framed together.
Top-down shot of three to four jar candles with pale blue and cream leftover wax and multiple wicks, plus an embossed ceramic lid resting among them on the bedsheet.
Overhead angle on the same group of part-used glass candles, tilting to show the wax surfaces, a lavender-pattern label, and the decorative lid laid on the white cloth.

windows.transcript: sort of fire. So just make sure that you're keeping your eye on this. I'm trying the centred as best as I can and you'll start to see around the rim how
windows.embed_text_v2: melting candle wax in glass jar using improvised double boiler on gas stove, saucepan with simmering water, metal wick-centering bar, wax turning clear around rim, melting stage
windows.summary_v2: Wax is being melted inside a glass jar set into a saucepan of simmering water on a gas stove, the makeshift double-boiler approach, with solid wax gradually liquefying around the rim.

tags_v2.action: melting wax via water-bath in jar

seconds_v2.desc: Stainless steel saucepan of water on a gas stove with a clear glass candle jar sitting inside it, being set up as an improvised double boiler.
A metal wick-centering tool/bar is lowered down into the glass jar resting in the water bath.
The metal centering bar sits across the rim of the glass jar in the saucepan, holding wicks in position.
Close overhead into the glass jar showing solid white wax around two metal wick tabs at the bottom, surrounded by simmering water.
Same close overhead, wax just starting to develop a thin melted ring around the edge as water bubbles around the jar.
Close into the jar, melted wax ring around the perimeter widens slightly while solid wax remains in the center.
Continued close overhead, melted ring of clear wax growing around still-white solid puck inside the glass jar.
Tight overhead, more of the wax has gone clear/liquid around the edges; wick tabs visible through the melt.
Even closer overhead into the jar, most of the visible wax around the perimeter has melted, only a central plug of solid wax remains.

windows.transcript: carefully pouring that into my little bowl. And this is what it looks like. I love this because I'm able to burn the leftover wax in my room
windows.embed_text_v2: showing leftover melted wax pooled in a small gold-rimmed glass vessel and burner, dark amber-brown molten wax, brass rim, chrome tealight reflector base, white tabletop, overhead pan
windows.summary_v2: A top-down camera pans around a small gold-rimmed glass burner holding leftover dark amber melted wax, ending on the empty vessel's tealight reflector base.

seconds_v2.desc: Top-down close-up of a small gold-rimmed glass vessel holding a pool of glossy dark amber-brown melted wax, set on a white surface.
Camera pans across the gold-rimmed bowl; the dark liquid wax fills it nearly to the brim with a reflective surface.
View shifts to the upper edge of the vessel, showing the thick brass-gold rim and the brown wax pooled inside.
Slightly angled overhead shot of the gold-rimmed container, dark wax inside catching a highlight, white tabletop around it.
Camera moves so the vessel sits in the right of frame, gold rim and brown molten wax visible, empty white surface to the left.
The vessel pushes to the upper-right corner, mostly out of frame, casting a soft shadow on the white surface as the camera glides past.
Camera returns to a near top-down view of the gold-rimmed glass holding dark amber wax, the pool smooth and reflective.
Overhead shot of the gold-rimmed bowl with brown molten wax inside, light reflecting off the brass rim and the wax surface.
Angled view into the now-empty gold-rimmed glass burner, revealing a chrome tealight reflector cup at its base where leftover wax is melted.
